title: How to Keep Shipping When You Walk Away from Your Desk — Zack Proser, WorkOS
summary: Zack Proser argues that human attention is the hard constraint in agent-based development, not tool speed. He outlines a four-layer architecture: signal agents that monitor Slack and Linear, verification gates spanning lint to browser interaction, weekly self-improvement loops over conversation history, and health integration via Oura ring to maintain developer well-being during asynchronous agent execution.
writeup: Zack Proser of WorkOS argues that human attention, not tool speed, is the binding constraint in agent-based development, and he lays out a four-layer architecture for shipping while away from the desk. The layers are: signal agents that monitor Slack and Linear, verification gates spanning lint checks to browser interaction, weekly self-improvement loops that mine conversation history, and health integration via Oura ring to track developer well-being during asynchronous agent runs. The takeaway is a concrete design for offloading work to agents while gating their output and preserving the engineer's focus.
